category_title: Adolescents with ASD exhibiting attention-seeking out-of-seat and assaultive behaviors
minimal_context: Brian is working with his teacher during morning lessons to complete five academic programmes while his peers are present at their tables.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Brian, it is time for our morning lessons. We need to work successfully through these five academic programmes.

STUDENT: (Stops working on the difficult task and leaves the table without permission)

TUTOR: Please sit back down and keep trying.

STUDENT: (Walks to the door, looking back to see if the teacher is watching) I made the wrong choice.

TUTOR: We have to finish the programmes.

STUDENT: (Approaches a peer at their table and exhibits physically abusive behaviour against them)

options.A: Maintain a ratio of praise to correction at 3:1 or 4:1 while the student works through the academic programmes during the morning lessons.
options.B: Provide an endless supply of academic programmes to work on, basing success on completing five in a row rather than the overall percentage correct.
options.C: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the intervention content while the student works through the academic programmes during the morning lessons.
options.D: Work with the student at the start of each day to agree on targets, requiring him to work successfully through five academic programmes before accessing the computer.
